children.name: Callaeas
children.description: The kokakos — a genus of large, arboreal passerines endemic to New Zealand, with grey plumage and striking blue or blue-black wattles on the face. They are largely frugivorous, with a specialised gizzard that helps process tough forest fruits. Their haunting, organ-like duetting song carries through the forest and is considered among the most beautiful of any New Zealand bird.

children.name: Philesturnus
children.description: The saddlebacks — a genus of striking black-and-chestnut birds endemic to New Zealand, named for the saddle-like chestnut patch across the back. They are highly active insectivores that forage on tree trunks and branches, probing bark crevices with their stout bills. They have been extensively translocated to predator-free islands and sanctuaries as a conservation measure.
